title: 2026 Subaru Uncharted - When Toyota & Subaru join forces.
description:
Full Review available at www.DriveChicago.com

2027 Subaru Uncharted
Quick, practical, and ready for EV adventures

Test Vehicle: 2026 Subaru Uncharted Sport
Powertrain: 74.7-kWh high-capacity lithium-ion battery
Exterior Color: Metropolis Gray Metallic
Interior Color: Gray
Options: Paint ($475)
MSRP as tested: $41,720 (With Delivery/Destination)

Pros: Range, Power, Fast-Charging, Standard Safety Features, AWD Availability
Cons: Tight Rear Seats, Digital Cluster Visibility, AWD Reduced Range

The 2026 Subaru Uncharted is an all-new,  all-electric subcompact crossover SUV jointly developed with Toyota. It's Subaru's version of the Toyota C-HR, though also closely related to the larger Subaru Solterra. The Uncharted is about 7 inches shorter than its EV sibling, making it a bit more nimble to drive. It is available in three trim levels known as Premium, Sport, and GT. 

It is available with two different powertrains, either a single motor or dual motor AWD. FWD models known as the base Premium trim are fitted with a front motor producing 221 horsepower via a 74.6 kWh battery pack. This marks Subaru's first front-wheel-drive vehicle in decades which is a notable departure for the all-wheel-drive brand. The Sport and GT trims use dual electric motors for a combined 338 horsepower and include Subaru Symmetrical All-Wheel Drive with X-MODE Dual-Mode System. 

Pricing starts at $34,995 for the base Premium FWD, $39,795 for the Sport, and $43,795 for the GT. It's Subaru's most affordable electric vehicle and marks the brand's entry into the subcompact EV segment. Key competitors include the Chevrolet Equinox EV, Ford Mustang Mach-E, Kia Niro EV, Nissan Leaf, and Toyota CH-R. 

The Uncharted is a great addition to the Subaru EV lineup. It offers an affordable entry point into electric vehicles that tends to edge out most of its competitors in features, performance and price. It has a unique design that it shares with the Toyota CH-R and has a respectable driving range (in particular for FWD). The technology inside works well and doesn’t require an engineering degree to use it. This new model is one to add to your shopping list if a compact EV is in your future.

title: The International Scout II is PERFECT except for some CRAZY FATAL FLAWS!
description:
When the American Dream seems to disappear like the sun in the West, we load our cars to the breaking point and chase it. Modern vehicles are designed for a world that works—they are dependent on proprietary parts, fluids, and software. They will stop rolling the moment the grid fails or the refineries shut down.

I’m looking for something that can adapt.

Welcome to the End of Days Report. My mission is to put fifteen classic 4-wheel drives through a grueling 17-point testing protocol to find the ultimate vehicle for a world where supplies are FOUND, not delivered.

In this episode, we’re evaluating the 1980 International Scout II. With its 345 cubic inch V8, Warner T-19 manual transmission, and Dana 300 transfer case, it looks like a contender. But how does it handle the realities of a post-collapse environment? From EMP-proofing to wood gasification potential, we’re running the numbers to see if the Scout II has what it takes to climb to the top of our leaderboard.

In this episode:

0:00 - The Reality of Rising Tides & Economic Collapse

1:30 - The 17-Point Testing Protocol Explained

2:45 - Identifying the "Apex" Scout II Drivetrain

4:20 - The Scorecard: Readiness, EMP-proofing, & Mobility

7:15 - Final Thoughts & The FTO Score

8:30 - Next Up: The Toyota FJ-40

Is the Scout II the survival rig you’ve been looking for, or does its mobility issue leave it dead in the water? Let me know in the comments.

Subscribe to the End of Days Report to follow the full leaderboard and see which classic 4x4 will claim the title of the ultimate survivor.

title: 🔋EV Scooter Vs ⛽️Petrol Scooter ⚔️மோதி பாக்கலாமா.! #shorts #electricscooter #scooter #olaev
description:
For Business Enquiry : collab@autozoom.in
For Shopping : http://www.autozoom.shop/

This short video compares EV scooters and petrol scooters. Petrol scooters are cheaper to buy but cost around ₹15,000 per year on fuel, while EVs cost only ₹4,000–₹6,000 yearly for charging. Petrol scooters need regular maintenance but are easier and cheaper to repair, with many mechanic shops available. EVs are almost maintenance-free but have expensive spare parts. Petrol scooters pollute the air, whereas EVs are cleaner in daily use, though battery production causes pollution. EVs offer smart features like GPS, music, and mobile-based locking, which petrol scooters lack. Petrol scooters have better resale value and can be towed easily if they run out of fuel—something EV scooters struggle with when the battery is drained.

description:
🔥काश वो लेता😱| Tata harrier ev | Harrier ev | Tata harrier ev ownership review | Harrier ev owner review | Adi zone #shorts 

Welcome to our detailed Tata Harrier EV ownership review, where we bring you an in-depth, real-world experience straight from an actual owner of the Tata Harrier EV 2025. If you're considering switching to an electric SUV or are curious about Tata’s latest all-electric offering, this video is your ultimate guide!

In this review, we dive deep into the everyday experience of owning and driving the Harrier EV — a vehicle that combines the bold styling of the legendary Harrier with the future-ready powertrain of Tata’s EV ecosystem. From city commutes to highway runs, charging scenarios to long drives, and build quality to after-sales service — everything is covered from a true owner’s point of view.


🚙 Key Highlights of the Video:

🔹 Design & Road Presence:
The Harrier EV carries forward the strong and muscular design DNA of the ICE Harrier, with a futuristic EV twist — featuring a closed grille, LED lighting signature, and aerodynamic wheels that scream "next-gen".

🔹 Interior & Features:
A look inside the Harrier EV’s plush and spacious cabin, loaded with premium features like a panoramic sunroof, 12.3” touchscreen infotainment, digital instrument cluster, ventilated seats, wireless charging, and an advanced connected car suite.

🔹 Performance & Drive Experience:
Real-world driving impressions — instant torque, regenerative braking, city maneuverability, highway stability, and how the Harrier EV balances power and comfort.

🔹 Battery, Range & Charging:
We talk about the Harrier EV’s battery capacity, real-world range in different driving conditions, fast charging support (DC & AC), and how the owner manages daily charging routines at home and on the go.

🔹 Ride Quality & Comfort:
Suspension performance, ground clearance, space for long journeys, and passenger comfort levels — especially in rear seats — are explained in depth.

🔹 Ownership Cost & Reliability:
Insights into maintenance cost, service experience with Tata, reliability of the EV platform, and how it compares to diesel or petrol Harrier in terms of running cost.

🔹 Tech & Safety:
The latest tech additions, including ADAS Level 2 features, 360-degree camera, lane keep assist, blind spot monitoring, and more. Plus, we discuss Tata’s build quality and expected safety ratings.

🔹 Pros & Cons:
What the owner loves and what could be improved — honest opinions on Tata’s EV offering in this segment.


---

✅ Who Should Watch This Video?
– EV buyers comparing options in the ₹25-30 lakh range
– Harrier fans curious about the electric version
– Tata EV enthusiasts
– First-time EV buyers
– Long-term EV owners considering an upgrade


🎯 Final Verdict:
Is the Tata Harrier EV worth the hype and the price tag? Does it deliver on Tata’s promise of a bold, electric, and practical SUV? Watch till the end for the owner’s final thoughts, buying advice, and whether this EV SUV makes sense in 2025!

title: MG Windsor EV vs Tata Curvv EV Sub Rs. 19 Lakh Battle | Which Car Is More Value For Money?
description:
We compare the closely priced variants of the MG Windsor EV and the Tata Curvv EV to find out which one of the two cars you should buy. In this video we will be comparing the electric automatic transmission variants of these cars. MG Windsor EV Essence Pro 0.0L is priced at ₹18.39 lakh and Tata Curvv EV Accomplished 45 0.0L is priced at ₹18.49 lakh. The prices mentioned are ex-showroom. This video helps you determine which car offers the best value for your money.

V3Cars compares the Value and Cost-Effectiveness of cars over time. It considers the size, performance, unique features, on-road price, maintenance cost and fuel cost.

Windsor EV’s 0.0-litre  electric engine makes 136PS of maximum power & 200Nm of peak torque. On the other hand, Curvv EV is available with a 0.0-litre  electric engine that makes 150PS of maximum power and 215Nm of peak torque. 

The Windsor EV is 4295mm long, 1850mm wide and 1677mm tall. The Windsor EV has a 2700mm long wheelbase. In comparison, the Curvv EV is 4310mm long, 1810mm wide and 1637mm tall. The Curvv EV has a 2560mm long wheelbase. 

Bigger exterior dimensions give a car a stronger road presence. A long wheelbase makes the car more stable at high speeds and gives better legroom in the back seat, while a shorter wheelbase makes the car more agile.

To help you make an informed decision, we also delve into maintenance costs and fuel efficiency. This video is presented in Hindi with some English terms, ensuring clarity and understanding.
Do not miss out on this comprehensive comparison! Watch now and decide which car aligns best with your needs and preferences.

title: Porsche Taycan za 1/5 ceny czyli walka Xiaomi z Huawei. który producent to wygra? Na pewno nie 🇩🇪
description:
Porsche Taycan za jedną piątą ceny i na dodatek lepszy — bo jeździ sam, ładuje się w 10 minut i ma technologię od Huawei.

Nowy SAIC Z7 powstał we współpracy koncernu SAIC (tego od MG) oraz Huawei i jest kolejnym dowodem na to, jak szybko Chińczycy przestali gonić… a zaczęli wyznaczać tempo. I choć w tytule pojawia się Porsche Taycan, to prawdziwa walka toczy się gdzie indziej.

Tu chodzi o starcie gigantów technologicznych: Huawei vs Xiaomi.

Bo największym rywalem Z7 wcale nie jest Porsche, tylko Xiaomi SU7. Dwa auta, bardzo podobna koncepcja, zupełnie inne podejście do technologii.

I teraz pytanie do Was: 👉 kto to wygra?

Na koniec szybkie porównanie 👇

SAIC Z7 (Huawei): – duża limuzyna klasy premium
– system Huawei ADS (zaawansowana autonomia)
– ultraszybkie ładowanie (ok. 10–15 min)
– bardzo mocne wersje AWD

Xiaomi SU7: – bardziej sportowy charakter
– nacisk na osiągi i software od Xiaomi
– bardzo szybkie ładowanie (800V)
– topowe wersje ponad 600–700 KM

Dwie filozofie. Jeden kierunek.

I serio — patrząc na tempo, to już nie jest pytanie „czy”, tylko „kiedy” Europa zacznie mieć realny problem.

👉 Xiaomi czy Huawei? Który system wygrywa?

title: I drove these NEW electric cars until they DIED!
description:
Save up to £3750 on an EV with the Electric Car Grant: https://bit.ly/Electric-Car-Grant-2025

It’s time for an all-new EV range test! 

We’ve got our hands on some of the latest all-electric SUVs, and we’re going to compare them to not only see which will go the furthest, but also how close each of them will get to their claimed range! 

The contenders we have today are the Genesis GV70, BMW iX, Polestar 3, Audi Q6 e-tron, and the Kia EV9. Let’s take a closer look at the contenders. 

The Genesis GV70 rolls up with an 80kWh battery pack and two electric motors. These combine to produce 490hp and 700Nm, and the GV70 has a claimed range of 298 miles. Picking one up new costs around £66,000. 

The BMW iX also has two motors, and they combine to produce 544hp and 765Nm. It has a larger battery than the GV70, though, at 109kWh, so as a result it has a much larger claimed range - 426 miles. It costs a whole lot more, though, with the car we have for this video costing £107,000! 

The Audi Q6 e-tron starts from £61,000, but for the exact car we have here, it costs from £71,000! Unlike the previous two cars, it only has one electric motor, and this produces 306hp and 485Nm. It has a claimed range of 360 miles, and a 95kWh battery pack.

Up next is the Kia EV9. The exact car we have here only costs from £65,000, and like the Audi it only has a single motor which is good for 203hp and 350Nm. It’s also equipped with a 100kWh battery, and this is good for a claimed range of 349 miles.

Then, finally, we have the Polestar 3. The car here is the entry-level model, which starts from around £70,000, but it has some optional extras added to it, so this car here costs around £85,000. It’s equipped with a single motor for 300hp and 490Nm. There’s a 111kWh battery pack, which gives the Polestar a claimed range of 438 miles. 

So which will travel furthest - the BMW, or the Polestar? Or could one of the other cars spring a surprise? You’ll need to stick with Mat to see for yourself!

title: NEW Toyota RAV4 vs Hyundai Tucson Hybrid Comparison Review: Finding The Ultimate Family SUV
description:
Toyota has finally lobbed the new RAV4. It’s hybrid only, sits on the same (though updated) platform with minimal dimension changes, while offering less power and bringing price hikes across the range. So is it really better than the car it replaces?

In short, yes, but we already know that. We compare the new 2026 Toyota RAV4 to the current best-selling hybrid medium SUV, the Hyundai Tucson in Hybrid Elite guise with N Line package. Both are petrol-electric, sitting just shy of $50,000 before on-road costs with space for five onboard.

The Tucson previously beat the last-gen RAV4, but things have changed. Editor Jez Spinks and road test editor John Law follow-up the Toyota’s launch in picturesque Tasmania with a twin-test to find out.

title: Harrier EV vs XEV 9E #harrierev #xev9e #comparison
description:
Tata Harrier EV vs Mahindra XEV 9E which one do you prefer?

Here is a quick comparison between Harrier EV Vs Mahindra XEV 9E.
- Harrier is wider and Taller, but XEV is Long and larger wheelbase
- Boot of XEV 9e is 160L more  and frunk is 115L more
- Harrier Battery pack has 65 KWH and 75 KWH (Claimed range 627KM)
- Harrier EV Power 390 BHP and 504 NM
- XEV9E battery pack has 59KWH and 79KWH
- XEV 9E Power 282 BHP and 380 NM

Clearly Harrier EV is more powerful and comes with ALL Wheel Drive and XEV with RWD
Harrier EV 0-100 in 6.3 and XEV 9E 6.8 seconds, Harrier EV is quick.
Harrier EV range is 627 KM and XEV 9E is 656 KM (Claimed range)

The Harrier EV comes with a larger 14.53-inch touchscreen infotainment system, which Tata claims is a first in the automotive space. On the other hand, the XEV 9e offers a three 12.3-inch Super Screens setup stretching across the width of the dashboard.

Is EV really cheaper than Petrol or Diesel in India? Or is it just hype?

In this detailed 5-Year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) breakdown, we compare Electric Vehicles vs Petrol vs Diesel SUVs (₹15–18 lakh segment) using real 2025 Indian numbers no opinions, only hard financial data.

We calculate everything:

✔ EMI & Interest (5-Year Loan @ 9%)
✔ Fuel vs Electricity Cost (Home + Fast Charging)
✔ Maintenance Reality (Indian conditions)
✔ Insurance Premium Differences
✔ Depreciation & Resale Value
✔ Break-even Analysis
✔ Apartment Users vs Home Charging Owners
✔ 8-Year Long-Term Ownership Scenario

Using realistic assumptions like ₹100/liter petrol, ₹90 diesel, ₹8/kWh home charging and ₹18/kWh fast charging, we reveal:

• Which vehicle is cheapest at 12,000 km/year
• When EV becomes financially smarter
• Why depreciation hurts EV resale
• Why diesel still wins in certain use cases
• When petrol makes more sense
• And who should actually buy what

If you're planning to buy a compact SUV in 2026, this video will save you lakhs.

This is not an emotional EV debate.
This is a spreadsheet-driven decision.

Watch till the end to understand the exact break-even point where EV overtakes petrol and diesel in India.

title: New 2026 Suzuki e-Vitara Vs 2026 Hyundai Creta EV The Brand's First Fully EV Comparison
description:
The #2026-Suzuki-e-Vitara and the #2026-Hyundai-Creta-EV mark a major shift for both brands as their first global, mass-market electric SUVs. While the #e-Vitara is a "born-electric" vehicle built on a dedicated EV platform (Heartect-e), the #Creta-EV is an electric conversion of the popular internal combustion engine (ICE) Creta.
Key Features:
#Suzuki Highlights: 10-way power-adjustable driver seat, 18-inch wheels, 7 airbags, and "Snow" terrain mode.
#Hyundai Highlights: Panoramic sunroof, dual-zone climate control, "Boss Mode" (moving the front passenger seat from the rear), and Vehicle-to-Load (V2L) technology, which allows you to power external appliances from the car's battery.
Verdict:
The #Suzuki-e-Vitara is arguably the better choice for those seeking a dedicated EV platform with genuine off-road capability (AWD) and a lower entry price. The #Hyundai-Creta-EV is geared toward buyers who prioritize premium features, faster charging, and the familiarity and interior space of the standard Creta.
Price and Variants:
Maruti #Suzuki-e-Vitara:
Price: ₹15.99 Lakh to ₹20.01 Lakh.
BaaS: ₹10.99 Lakh + ₹3.99/km.
3 Variants: Delta, Zeta, Alpha.
#Hyundai-Creta Electric:
Price: ₹18.02 L to ₹24.40 L.
Variants: Executive, Smart, Premium, Excellence
Verdict: The prices of the Maruti #Suzuki-e-Vitara makes it highly affordable, compared to the #Hyundai-Creta-EV.
Final Selection Guide:
Choose the #Suzuki-e-Vitara if: You want the lowest possible entry price via BaaS, require AWD for light off-roading or snowy conditions, or value a dedicated EV platform with a longer wheelbase.
Choose the #Hyundai-Creta-EV if: You want a "luxury-first" experience with a panoramic sunroof, faster DC charging, V2L power-sharing, and a much larger boot for family road trips.

title: 2026 Kia EV4 vs  Ford Mustang Mach E Which One Stands Out More?
description:
Sleek electric fastback or muscular electric crossover? Which 2026 EV stands out more: The Kia EV4 or the Ford Mustang Mach-E? Let me know your thoughts! 👇🇰🇷vs🇺🇸

In this highly detailed 2026 Kia EV4 vs. Ford Mustang Mach-E POV Drive & Comparison, we take two of the most striking electric vehicles on the market and put them head-to-head. Both of these cars are designed to turn heads and offer incredible daily drivability, but they take completely different approaches to the EV experience.

First, we jump into the all-new 2026 Kia EV4. Kia threw out the traditional sedan rulebook to create a sleek, aerodynamic fastback. Powered by an 81.4 kWh Long Range battery, the EV4 targets a massive 330 miles of range. We test out the ultra-modern, minimalist cabin featuring dual 12.3-inch panoramic displays, physical climate buttons, and the incredibly smooth, efficient 201 HP front-wheel-drive motor in city traffic.  

Next, we swap into the heavily updated 2026 Ford Mustang Mach-E. Ford kept the aggressive muscle-SUV styling but added some serious upgrades for 2026, including a standard heat pump across all trims to maximize winter range! We test the Mach-E's raw power, feeling the punch of its dual-motor eAWD system (capable of up to 480 HP and 700 lb-ft of torque on GT models). We also explore the massive 15.5-inch vertical center touchscreen and test Ford's BlueCruise hands-free driving on the highway.

title: 스타리아 캠핑카에 적합한 스타리아 EV 카고 vs 투어러? 안보고 사면 무조건 후회 합니다
description:
안녕하세요 휴먼캠핑카 입니다.  스타리아 ev  출시로 어떤 차량을 선택할지 문의가 많이 오고 있습니다. 
보조금이랑 구조 등을 고려하여 설명드렸습니다. 
 1. 2026 스타리아 EV: 카고 vs 투어러 선택 가이드
고객분들이 가장 많이 묻는 보조금과 제작 효율 측면에서의 비교입니다.

스타리아 EV 카고 (3인승/5인승):

보조금 혜택: 소형 전기 화물차로 분류되어 1800만 원~ 2100만원까지 지원받을 수 있어 실구매가 메리트가 매우 큽니다.

제작 강점: 내장재가 없는 상태라 휴먼캠핑카만의 가로침상이나 천고를 높여 최대한 공간 활용이 가능합니다

스타리아 EV 투어러 (9인승/11인승):

보조금: 승합 보조금이 적용되며 최대 2200만언~ 2300만원까지 보조금을 받을 수 있습니다.

구조적 이점: 대형 통유리가 기본이라 개방감이 좋고, 멀티링크 서스펜션 덕분에 가족 단위 고객이 선호하는 부드러운 승차감을 제공합니다.

하지만 차량대수가 한정적이라 보조금을 받기 어려울 수 있습니다. 

충전 효율: 스타리아 EV는 800V 초고속 충전 시스템을 지원하여 10%에서 80%까지 약 20분이면 충전이 가능합니다.

전기차로 차박을 해보니  특히 전력 사용이 많은 장박 캠핑 시 충전 시설을 자주 찾아야 하는 번거로움이 있을 수 있어 라이프 스타일 고려하여 차량을 선택하시면 됩니다. 

3. 구조 변경 시 주의사항 (보조금 관련)

보조금에 대한 환수 부분은 없는 것으로 확인되었구요 부가세 경우에는 이동사무실 시 업태에 따라 환급여부가 결정되며 2년 후에 구조변경을 하게 되면 부가세 환급은 없습니다. 

💡 SNS 게시물 및 상담용 한 줄 정리
"경제성을 중시한다면 보조금 혜택이 큰 '카고'를, 승차감과 개방감을 원한다면 '투어러'를 추천합니다. 다만, 장박 위주라면 충전 스트레스가 적은 스타리아 EV가 PV5보다 유리한 선택이 될 수 있습니다."

대표님의 풍부한 제작 경험과 직접 타보신 PV5의 후기가 더해진다면, 고객들이 본인의 라이프 스타일에 딱 맞는 차량을 선택하는 데 큰 확신을 가질 수 있을 것 같습니다!

title: EV vs Gas SHOWDOWN! Cadillac Escalade IQ vs Cadillac Escalade SUV Comparison - Which One Is Better?
description:
Cadillac Escalade IQ Sport vs Cadillac Escalade Platinum Sport SUV Comparison - Which One Is Better?

The 2026 Cadillac Escalade IQ Sport in Black Raven is a stunning blend of cutting-edge electric performance and flagship SUV luxury. This all-electric full-size SUV boasts dual motors with a Cadillac-estimated 750 horsepower and 785 pound-feet of torque when you engage “Velocity Max,” giving it straight-line performance that rivals many performance SUVs while still providing the quiet, refined ride you expect in a luxury vehicle. Underneath its commanding design, the Escalade IQ Sport features a massive 205 kWh battery pack, offering up to an estimated 465-mile range, advanced 4-wheel steering for enhanced maneuverability, and an air ride adaptive suspension with magnetic ride control that keeps every drive composed. Inside, the Esclade IQ Sport is wrapped in premium materials with super-wide digital displays, modern tech features like Super Cruise hands-free driving assistance, and seating for up to seven passengers in three rows, making it both a family hauler and a high-tech statement on wheels. Cadillac-estimated figures include the expansive interior volume and the flagship level of powertrain refinement unique to the Sport trim on this electric Escalade platform. 

The 2026 Cadillac Escalade Platinum Sport ESV in Black Raven is the ultimate expression of Cadillac’s traditional luxury SUV pedigree. Powered by a robust 6.2-liter V8 gasoline engine, the Platinum Sport ESV delivers 420 horsepower and 460 pound-feet of torque, paired with a smooth 10-speed automatic transmission and a four-wheel-drive system that gives this extended-length SUV confident capability on a variety of road surfaces. Built on the Escalade ESV platform, this version expands cargo space and rear passenger comfort while maintaining the bold, aggressive exterior cues of the Sport trim, including gloss black accents, a sport grille, and 24-inch alloy wheels. The Platinum Sport ESV also elevates the experience with luxurious appointments such as premium leather seating with heating and ventilation, a high-end audio system, power retractable assist steps, and available touring enhancements that make long trips supremely comfortable. From its sculpted body in Black Raven to its fortified towing capacity and spacious rear cabin, the Platinum Sport ESV blends classic SUV capability with premium refinement. 

When comparing the 2026 Cadillac Escalade IQ Sport to the 2026 Cadillac Escalade Platinum Sport ESV, you’re looking at two very different interpretations of the modern luxury SUV that both deliver strong value in today’s market. The Escalade IQ Sport represents Cadillac’s electrified future, combining futuristic all-electric performance with a long estimated range, innovative tech features, and a unique driving experience that’s smooth, powerful, and quiet. Its dual-motor setup and high torque output make it exceptionally quick for its size, and the Sport trim brings plenty of premium features to the plate. On the other hand, the Platinum Sport ESV sticks with a traditional V8 powerplant that has long defined the Escalade’s character — delivering dependable power, familiar mechanical performance, and an extended-length body that’s perfect for families and gear-heavy lifestyles. The Platinum Sport ESV’s classic gasoline engine, refined cabin, and commanding presence make it a strong choice for buyers who want comfort and capability without transitioning fully to electric. Both SUVs offer iconic Cadillac luxury, advanced technology, and strong road manners, but your choice boils down to whether you prioritize the thrilling innovation of an electric powerhouse or the tried-and-true presence and flexibility of a high-end gasoline SUV. Regardless of the path you choose, each model brings its own set of strengths that make them compelling buys in a competitive full-size luxury SUV market.

title: Thermal Truth Seen !
description:
Watch gas cars glow with wasted heat, while EVs stay cool and efficient 🤯🌡️

A thermal camera reveals how internal combustion vehicles lose large amounts of energy as heat, especially around the engine bay, radiator, and exhaust system.

Gas engines burn fuel to create motion, but much of that energy escapes as unwanted heat instead of reaching the wheels.

Electric vehicles work differently. Their motors convert a much higher share of stored energy into movement, so they often appear cooler on thermal imaging during similar use.

That lower heat loss can improve efficiency, reduce wasted energy, and simplify many cooling demands.

It’s a powerful visual example of why EV drivetrains are changing modern transportation.

title: Tata Harrier Ev vs Mahindra BE 6 | Real comparison! No hype!
description:
Tata Harrier Ev vs Mahindra BE 6 | Real comparison! No hype!

अगर आप EV की दुनिया में धमाल मचाने की प्लानिंग कर रहे हैं, तो ज़रूर ये वीडियो देखिए! 🚗⚡

इस वीडियो में हम ले कर आए हैं Tata Harrier EV और Mahindra BE 6 का दमदार मुकाबला — पावर, परफॉर्मेंस, स्पेस, स्टाइल, चार्जिंग और फीचर्स सभी एंगल्स से। जानिए क्यों Harrier EV का ड्यूल मोटर AWD वैरिएंट रॉ थ्रिल और ऑफ‑रोड मांसपावर में सबसे आगे खड़ी है, और क्या BE 6 का कूप-स्पोर्टी लुक, बड़ा फ्रंक, बेहतर चार्जिंग और फ्यूचरिस्टिक केबिन आपकी शहरी जिंदगी के लिए बेस्ट चॉइस हो सकती है। इस वीडियो में शामिल है:

🔹 Dimensional showdown
🔹 Battery & real-world range comparison
🔹 Powerplay: bhp & torque face‑off
🔹 Charging ecosystems tested
🔹 Feature highlights & cabin experience
🔹 Build-quality & safety verdict

तो अगर आप “टफ एंड टॉर्क… या टेक और लग्ज़री?” में उलझे हुए हैं, तो यह वीडियो आपकी क्विक गाइड बन सकती है! 👊🔥

ऑल-व्हील ड्राइव (AWD): क्या Harrier EV का ये फीचर आपको ज़्यादा ऑफ-रोड एडवेंचर की ओर आकर्षित करता है?

डायमेंशन और स्पेस: क्या आप थोड़ी मस्कुलर लेआउट (Harrier EV) में अधिक कंफर्ट पसंद करेंगे, या लंबा व्हीलबेस और बूट स्पेस (BE 6) को प्राथमिकता देंगे?

रेंज के मामले में एफिशिएंसी: क्या BE 6 की बैटरी एफिशिएंसी आपको Harrier EV से कहीं ज़्यादा आकर्षित करती है?

परफॉर्मेंस और पावर: क्या 390 bhp और 504 Nm torque वाले Harrier EV AWD वेरिएंट का ड्राइविंग एक्सपीरियंस आप देखने के लिए उत्साहित हैं?

चार्जिंग स्पीड: क्या आपको BE 6 के 180 kW DC & 11.2 kW AC चार्जिंग पॉइंट ज़्यादा दिलचस्प लगते हैं, खासकर लंबी दूरी के सफरों के लिए?

स्टाइल और लुक: आप कौनसा लुक ज़्यादा पसंद करेंगे — Robotic, muscular SUV जैसा Harrier EV या futuristic coupe-स्पोर्टी BE 6?

प्रैक्टिकल फीचर्स: क्या BE 6 के फ्लश डोर हैंडल्स, फैब्रिक सनरूफ, और ड्राइवर ड्रोसिनेस डिटेक्शन जैसे एडवांस्ड फीचर्स आपके दिल को छू जाते हैं?

इंटीरियर टेक और साउंड सिस्टम: क्या आपको Harrier EV के बड़े 14.5" Q‑LED स्क्रीन और JBL सिस्टम पसंद हैं, या BE 6 की Sci‑fi ग्लो और Harman Kardon 16 स्पीकर आपको लगते हैं सही चॉइस?

V2L/V2B और ऑफ‑रोड मोड्स: क्या आपको Harrier EV का ये मोबाइल पावरहाउस फीचर — जैसे उपकरणों को चार्ज करना या ऑफ़‑रोड मदद — ज़्यादा उपयोगी लगेगा?

बिल्ड क्वालिटी और सेफ्टी: क्या Harrier EV का टैंक‑जैसा बिल्ड और 5‑स्टार क्रैश रेटिंग आपको मन को पूरा दिलासा देती है, या BE 6 भी काफी भरोसेमंद साबित होती है?

Watch now और कमेंट करके बताओ — कौन सी EV बन गई आपकी #1 पसंद?
👇👇👇

title: India's 3rd Car Brand!! JSW Motors Strategy REVEALED! 🔥Jetour T2, Jaecoo J5, Jaecoo J7 & iCar V23
description:
Jetour T2, Jaecoo J5, J7 & iCar V23 🔥 JSW Motors India Strategy REVEALED!!

📩 For Brand Collaborations & Sponsorships – rudrarajpawar7@gmail.com

📌 About This Video:

JSW Motors India is entering the car market in a BIG way — and this is not just another launch.
With global partnership from Chery Automobile, JSW is planning a full SUV lineup in India — from premium hybrid SUVs to affordable electric SUVs.

In this video, I break down the complete India strategy of JSW Motors, covering the Jetour T2, Jaecoo J5, iCar V23, and Jaecoo J7, along with their expected pricing, features, range, and real competition in India.

From a Defender-inspired hybrid SUV to a Range Rover-style EV, and even a Thar-like electric SUV — this lineup is seriously interesting.

🔍 In This Video, You’ll Get:

✅ Jetour T2 India launch details, pricing & hybrid system explained
✅ Jaecoo J5 EV SUV segment breakdown (20–25 lakh range)
✅ iCar V23 lifestyle EV SUV (Thar/Jimny rival) full details
✅ Jaecoo J7 hybrid SUV features, powertrain & segment positioning
✅ JSW Motors India strategy explained in simple Hinglish
✅ Comparison with real Indian rivals (Fortuner, Creta EV, Safari, XUV, Thar)
✅ My honest opinion — should you wait for these SUVs?

🚙 Cars Covered in This Video:
Jetour T2 (JSW SUV)
Jaecoo J5 (EV SUV)
iCar V23 (Lifestyle EV SUV)
Jaecoo J7 (Hybrid SUV)
🔥 Detailed Comparison & Positioning:
🟩 Jetour T2 (Hybrid SUV)

The Jetour T2 is a rugged, boxy SUV inspired by the Land Rover Defender and designed to challenge premium SUVs in India.
The Jetour T2 comes with a plug-in hybrid system, combining petrol + electric driving.

Jetour T2 vs competitors:

Toyota Fortuner
Mahindra Scorpio-N
MG Gloster (Majestor segment)
Jeep Wrangler
Land Rover Defender

If priced right, Jetour T2 could be a serious alternative to Fortuner and Defender-style SUVs.

⚡ Jaecoo J5 (EV SUV)

The Jaecoo J5 is a premium-looking electric SUV inspired by the Range Rover Sport design philosophy.

Jaecoo J5 will compete in the mid-range EV SUV segment.

Jaecoo J5 vs competitors:

Tata Harrier EV
Mahindra XEV 9e

With ~450–500 km range, Jaecoo J5 can be a strong alternative in the EV SUV market.

🧊 iCar V23 (Lifestyle EV SUV)

The iCar V23 is a compact, boxy electric SUV with strong off-road styling.

iCar V23 vs competitors:

Mahindra Thar
Maruti Suzuki Jimny
Mahindra Vision S (upcoming EV SUV concept)

The iCar V23 targets young buyers who want a stylish, rugged EV SUV with lifestyle appeal.

title: Tata Harrier Ev vs Mahindra Xev 9e | Real comparison! No hype!
description:
Tata Harrier Ev vs Mahindra Xev 9e | Real comparison! No hype!

अगर आप EV की दुनिया में धमाल मचाने की प्लानिंग कर रहे हैं, तो ज़रूर ये वीडियो देखिए! 🚗⚡

इस वीडियो में हम ले कर आए हैं Tata Harrier EV और Mahindra XEV9 का दमदार मुकाबला — पावर, परफॉर्मेंस, स्पेस, स्टाइल, चार्जिंग और फीचर्स सभी एंगल्स से। जानिए क्यों Harrier EV का ड्यूल मोटर AWD वैरिएंट रॉ थ्रिल और ऑफ‑रोड मांसपावर में सबसे आगे खड़ी है, और क्या XEV9 का कूप-स्पोर्टी लुक, बड़ा फ्रंक, बेहतर चार्जिंग और फ्यूचरिस्टिक केबिन आपकी शहरी जिंदगी के लिए बेस्ट चॉइस हो सकती है। इस वीडियो में शामिल है:

🔹 Dimensional showdown
🔹 Battery & real-world range comparison
🔹 Powerplay: bhp & torque face‑off
🔹 Charging ecosystems tested
🔹 Feature highlights & cabin experience
🔹 Build-quality & safety verdict

तो अगर आप “टफ एंड टॉर्क… या टेक और लग्ज़री?” में उलझे हुए हैं, तो यह वीडियो आपकी क्विक गाइड बन सकती है! 👊🔥

ऑल-व्हील ड्राइव (AWD): क्या Harrier EV का ये फीचर आपको ज़्यादा ऑफ-रोड एडवेंचर की ओर आकर्षित करता है?

डायमेंशन और स्पेस: क्या आप थोड़ी मस्कुलर लेआउट (Harrier EV) में अधिक कंफर्ट पसंद करेंगे, या लंबा व्हीलबेस और बूट स्पेस (XEV9) को प्राथमिकता देंगे?

रेंज के मामले में एफिशिएंसी: क्या XEV9 की बैटरी एफिशिएंसी आपको Harrier EV से कहीं ज़्यादा आकर्षित करती है?

परफॉर्मेंस और पावर: क्या 390 bhp और 504 Nm torque वाले Harrier EV AWD वेरिएंट का ड्राइविंग एक्सपीरियंस आप देखने के लिए उत्साहित हैं?

चार्जिंग स्पीड: क्या आपको XEV9 के 180 kW DC & 11.2 kW AC चार्जिंग पॉइंट ज़्यादा दिलचस्प लगते हैं, खासकर लंबी दूरी के सफरों के लिए?

स्टाइल और लुक: आप कौनसा लुक ज़्यादा पसंद करेंगे — Robotic, muscular SUV जैसा Harrier EV या futuristic coupe-स्पोर्टी XEV9?

प्रैक्टिकल फीचर्स: क्या XEV9 के फ्लश डोर हैंडल्स, फैब्रिक सनरूफ, और ड्राइवर ड्रोसिनेस डिटेक्शन जैसे एडवांस्ड फीचर्स आपके दिल को छू जाते हैं?

इंटीरियर टेक और साउंड सिस्टम: क्या आपको Harrier EV के बड़े 14.5" Q‑LED स्क्रीन और JBL सिस्टम पसंद हैं, या XEV9 की Sci‑fi ग्लो और Harman Kardon 16 स्पीकर आपको लगते हैं सही चॉइस?

V2L/V2B और ऑफ‑रोड मोड्स: क्या आपको Harrier EV का ये मोबाइल पावरहाउस फीचर — जैसे उपकरणों को चार्ज करना या ऑफ़‑रोड मदद — ज़्यादा उपयोगी लगेगा?

बिल्ड क्वालिटी और सेफ्टी: क्या Harrier EV का टैंक‑जैसा बिल्ड और 5‑स्टार क्रैश रेटिंग आपको मन को पूरा दिलासा देती है, या XEV9 भी काफी भरोसेमंद साबित होती है?

Watch now और कमेंट करके बताओ — कौन सी EV बन गई आपकी #1 पसंद?

- Kaunsi battery sabse zyada safe aur efficient hai
- Charging cycles aur real-world battery degradation kitna hota hai

description:

So tell us — with 500km of real-world range in your pocket, would you make the switch to an EV or stick to your fuel-powered favourite? ⚡🚗

title: The Longest-Range Electric Cars You Can Buy in 2025 (Ranked)
description:
Range anxiety used to be the biggest excuse not to buy an electric car. In 2025, that excuse is officially retired.

In this video, I rank the five longest-range electric vehicles you can actually buy in the United States, counting down from number five to the current distance champion. These are not concept cars or engineering experiments. They are real, usable, long-distance EVs designed for people who drive far and expect their car to keep up.

From the ultra-efficient Mercedes-Benz EQS 450+, to the still-dominant Tesla Model S Long Range, and the range-defining lineup from Lucid Motors, this is where electric cars finally stop asking for compromise.

We look at real-world range, charging architecture, performance, interior execution, pricing, and why long range matters more now than ever as incentives fade and infrastructure struggles to keep up.

If you drive long distances, hate planning your life around charging stops, or just want the calm confidence of knowing your car will go farther than you do, this list matters.

I’m Nik Miles. Let’s talk about how far electric cars have actually come.

description:
Cheapest Electric Cars in India #evcar #shortsfeed #automobile #tiagoev  #mgcometev 

Cheapest Electric Cars in India (2025)
Vayve Mobility Eva - Starting Price: ₹3.25 lakh

Details: Noted as the cheapest electric car available in India in 2025. Specific details on battery capacity, range, and features are limited, but its low price makes it a standout for budget-conscious buyers seeking an entry-level EV.
Suitability: Likely designed for urban commuting, given its affordability and compact design.


Strom Motors R3 - Starting Price: ₹4.79 lakh

Battery and Range: Equipped with a battery offering a range of approximately 200 km on a single charge.
Features: A two-seater EV with a unique, futuristic two-door design, keyless entry, a 7-inch infotainment touchscreen, Bluetooth infotainment, and LED headlights. Its compact size makes it ideal for city driving and tight parking spaces.
Suitability: Perfect for urban commuters looking for an affordable, eco-friendly option with modern features.


PMV EaS E - Starting Price: ₹4.79 lakh

Battery and Range: Offers a range of 160 km, suitable for short city drives.
Features: Includes Bluetooth infotainment, LED headlights, and keyless entry. Its compact design caters to urban mobility.
Suitability: A budget-friendly choice for city dwellers prioritizing affordability and ease of use in congested areas.


MG Comet EV - Starting Price: ₹6.17 lakh - ₹6.99 lakh

Battery and Range: Powered by a 17.3 kWh Li-ion battery, delivering a range of 230 km on a single charge.
Features: A compact, two-door, four-seater hatchback with a 10.25-inch touchscreen infotainment system, wireless Android Auto and Apple CarPlay, a 10.25-inch digital driver’s display, connected car tech, and safety features like ABS, EBD, Hill Hold Control, and dual airbags. Its small size (2.9 meters long) and 4.2-meter turning radius make it ideal for city driving.
Suitability: Perfect for urban commuters seeking a feature-rich, compact EV at a low price. Its premium interior and tech make it a strong contender in the budget segment.


Tata Tiago EV - Starting Price: ₹7.99 lakh

Battery and Range: Available with two battery options: 19.2 kWh (250 km range) and 24 kWh (315 km range).
Features: A four-door, five-seater hatchback with a 7-inch touchscreen infotainment system, Apple CarPlay, Android Auto, an 8-speaker Harman sound system, automatic climate control, cruise control, and safety features like dual airbags, electronic stability control, and a reverse parking camera. title: Vayve Eva: India’s First Solar-Powered Electric Car Launched at ₹3.25 Lakh | Features & Details
description:
India has taken a significant leap in sustainable transportation with the launch of the Vayve Eva, the nation’s first solar-powered electric car. Unveiled at the Bharat Mobility Global Expo 2025, the Eva is set to revolutionize urban mobility with its compact design, eco-friendly technology, and affordability.  ￼

Design and Dimensions

The Vayve Eva is reminiscent of small EVs like the Mahindra E2O and Reva, featuring a compact hatchback design ideal for city commutes. It measures 2,950 mm in length and 1,200 mm in width, making it adept at navigating through congested urban areas. Despite its petite size, the Eva comfortably accommodates up to two adults and one child, providing a practical solution for small families or individual travelers.  ￼

Variants and Pricing

Vayve offers the Eva in three variants to cater to diverse customer needs:
 • Nova: Priced at ₹3.25 lakh (ex-showroom), this base model comes with a 9 kWh battery pack, delivering a real-world range of approximately 125 km.  ￼
 • Stella: At ₹3.99 lakh (ex-showroom), the Stella variant is equipped with a 12.6 kWh battery, offering a range of around 175 km.  ￼
 • Vega: The top-tier Vega variant is priced at ₹4.49 lakh (ex-showroom) and features an 18 kWh battery pack, providing an impressive range of up to 250 km on a single charge.  ￼

Solar Integration and Charging

A standout feature of the Eva is its solar roof, capable of providing an additional range of up to 10 km per day, translating to approximately 3,000 km of free driving annually. This solar integration reduces dependency on charging infrastructure, promoting a more sustainable mode of transportation. For conventional charging, the Eva supports DC fast charging, allowing the battery to reach 80% capacity in just 45 minutes.  ￼

Performance and Specifications

The Eva is powered by an electric motor delivering 20.11 bhp, ensuring efficient performance for urban driving. Its compact dimensions and lightweight design contribute to an operational cost of approximately ₹0.5 per km, making it an economical choice for daily commutes.  ￼

Interior and Features

Inside, the Eva is equipped with modern amenities to enhance the driving experience. Features include dual digital displays, a 6-way powered driver seat, and a fixed glass roof, providing a blend of comfort and convenience. The vehicle also offers smart connectivity options, including Android Auto and Apple CarPlay, ensuring drivers stay connected on the go.  ￼

Safety Features

Safety is a priority in the Eva’s design. The vehicle comes with a driver airbag and seatbelts for both passengers, providing essential protection during city drives. Additionally, the monocoque chassis and front crumple zone enhance the vehicle’s safety credentials.  ￼

Conclusion

The Vayve Eva represents a significant advancement in India’s electric vehicle landscape, offering an affordable, eco-friendly, and practical solution for urban transportation. Its combination of solar integration, compact design, and modern features positions it as a compelling choice for environmentally conscious consumers. With pre-launch bookings set to open in January 2025, the Eva is poised to make a substantial impact on the Indian automotive market.

title: Does AC Reduce Electric Car Range? Real EV Test & Tips to Maximize Range in 2025| Electric car facts
description:
In this YouTube Shorts video, we explore a question every electric car owner or buyer has — does using the air conditioner (AC) reduce an EV’s range? The answer is yes, and we’ll show you just how much! Whether you're driving a Tata Nexon EV, MG ZS EV, BYD Atto 3, or any other electric vehicle, AC usage can significantly impact battery consumption, especially in Indian weather conditions.

Electric cars rely on stored battery power for every function — including heating, cooling, lights, infotainment, and more. In hot summers, EV users tend to keep the AC running continuously, which can lead to a drop in real-world range by 10%–20%, depending on outside temperature, battery efficiency, and driving habits.

In this short, we share practical tips to help EV users minimize range loss due to air conditioning. You’ll learn about pre-cooling your EV while plugged in, choosing eco-mode settings, using ventilation instead of full AC, and how speed influences cooling demands.

Watch now and become a smarter electric vehicle driver! If you care about getting the best performance from your EV and avoiding range anxiety, this video is a must-watch in 2025.

title: I drove these new electric cars (and a Petrol V8) until they died
description:
Get 20% off Car Vertical using code Carwow: https://bit.ly/Carwow-Car-Vertical

Can some of the newest electric cars on sale in 2025 go far enough on a single charge? 

To find out, we’ve gathered six different cars, charged them all to 100% battery, and we’re going to see which can travel the furthest! But not only will we see which goes the furthest, we’ll also see how each car compares to its claimed maximum range on a single charge. 

For this video, we’ve got our hands on a variety of different cars. We have the new Tesla Model Y, the Kia EV6, and the Skoda Enyaq Coupe. We’ve also got our hands on three of the newest Chinese cars on sale in the UK - the BYD Sealion 7, the Xpeng G6, and the Leapmotor C10. 

All of the cars we have here are the longest range versions that are on sale today, and when looking at the claimed range, the Tesla should be winning this test… 

Claimed Ranges:
- Tesla Model Y: 387 miles
- Kia EV3: 375 miles
- Skoda Enyaq Coupe: 365 miles
- Xpeng G6: 354 miles
- BYD Sealion 7: 312 miles
- Leapmotor C10: 263 miles

But as we’ve found in previous range tests, some electric cars can struggle to come close to their claimed range! 

So the question is, will the Tesla hold on and travel the furthest? Or will one of the other cars snatch the win? You’ll need to stick with Mat and the team to find out for yourself!

title: ஒரே சார்ஜில் 550 கிமீ சாத்தியமா? Tata Harrier EV 100-0% Range test by Autotrend Tamil
description:
The #TataHarrierEV is available from ₹21.49L to ₹30.23L as the flagship SUV from Tata motors.

For test drives & bookings contact
The True Sai works, Tata Motors dealership
Salem, Dharmapuri, Krishnagiri, Hosur, Namakkal, Karur
90039 17271

Tata Harrier EV QWD full range test:
https://youtu.be/p4G6HnZqD68?si=0uaCp0bPGdxpdRo1

Tata Harrier QWD EV Detailed First Look Walkaround:
https://youtu.be/yBoEh9qGDRk?si=spz-Y5w0zd9eudDc

Key Highlights:
 * Performance:
   * Dual Motor Setup: Available with a dual-motor configuration for All-Wheel Drive (AWD).
   * Power & Torque: The front motor produces 158 PS (116 kW) and the rear motor 238 PS (175 kW), with a combined torque of 504 Nm.
   * Acceleration: 0-100 km/h in a segment-best 6.3 seconds (with Boost Mode in AWD variants).
 * Battery & Range:
   * Battery Options: Available with two battery packs: 65 kWh and 75 kWh (Lithium-Iron Phosphate technology).
   * Claimed Range: Up to 627 km (MIDC cycle) for the 75 kWh battery pack, translating to an estimated real-world range of 480-505 km.
   * Fast Charging: Supports 120 kW DC fast charging, allowing for 20-80% charge in just 25 minutes, and can gain up to 250 km of range in 15 minutes.
   * Home Charging: A 7.2 kW AC home charger can replenish the battery from 10-100% in 10.7 hours.
   * Lifetime Battery Warranty for unlimited kms.

 * Platform & Drivetrain:
   * Built on Tata's advanced acti.ev platform, which allows for both Rear-Wheel Drive (RWD) and All-Wheel Drive (AWD) configurations.

 * Advanced Features & Technology:
   * Infotainment: Features a 14.53-inch Harman QLED touchscreen infotainment system (world's first Samsung Neo QLED automotive display).
   * Sound System: 10-speaker JBL premium audio system with Dolby Atmos support.
   * 10.25-inch fully digital driver's display with integrated maps.
   * Safety: Comprehensive safety suite including 7 airbags, Level 2 ADAS with 20+ features, 540-degree surround view with "Transparent Mode", blind spot view monitor, and an HD Rear-view mirror (e-IRVM) with Digital Video Recorder.
   * Comfort & Convenience: Powered and ventilated front seats with memory function, powered BOSS mode for extended rear legroom, voice-assisted panoramic sunroof, dual-zone auto AC with rear vents, wireless Apple CarPlay/Android Auto, wireless phone charger, multi-color ambient lighting, and cooled storage under the armrest.
   * Smart Features: Digital key, auto-park assist with "Summon Mode" (call your car forward or reverse using a smartphone), "Reverse Assist" (retraces exact path taken in reverse), V2L (Vehicle-to-Load) and V2V (Vehicle-to-Vehicle) capabilities, and DrivePay integration for in-car EV charging payments.
   * Terrain Modes: Six intelligent terrain modes (Sand, Snow, Rock, Mud/Ruts, Normal, and Custom) and Off-Road Assist (low-speed cruise control).
   * Regenerative Braking: Four levels of regenerative braking.

 * Variants: Available in three variants: Adventure, Fearless, and Empowered, with a Stealth variant also listed.

When “design inspiration” crosses into copycat territory — you decide.

Surprisingly plush cabin, reclining seats and the biggest portrait screen in its class.

Not for enthusiasts, but smooth enough for daily drivers who want value over thrills.

3.6 mi/kWh efficiency, 80 kW DC max – average charging speeds vs faster rivals.

Zara bag or Hermès bag? Nicki’s analogy sums up the E5’s identity crisis perfectly.

Unbeatable price, good range, solid spec – just don’t expect Range Rover polish.

Test it, compare it, and if you’re not bothered by dynamics – enjoy saving £75K.

title: BYD Sealion 7 — Real-World Range Tested!
description:
We took the BYD Sealion 7 AWD from Gurgaon to Delhi with 97% battery and hit smooth speeds on NH8, even maintaining 80kmph consistently. Despite its 530PS power and 0-100kmph claim of 4.5 sec, it felt more like a luxurious cruiser than a hardcore performance SUV. From smooth touchscreen, stylish looks to comfortable rear seats — this car is all about premium feel. It used only 6% battery over 31km, giving us an estimated range of 517km. Confidently expect over 475km in real-world use!

Would this kind of range ease your EV anxiety? Tell us in the comments!

title: Tata Nexon EV vs MG Windsor EV!
description:
The Tata Nexon EV is a sub-4 metre electric SUV that has been extremely popular in the Indian market. It first went on sale in 2020 and later down the line, got a heavily revised facelift in 2023. The Nexon EV brings power, silence and lower running costs to what is an already capable car in its ICE avatar.

The MG Windsor EV is an electric crossover in India and offers the seating capacity for up to five people. The Windsor EV is the third all-electric from MG India after the ZS EV and Comet EV. It uses a 38 kWh battery and offers a claimed range of up to 331 km. The Windsor EV blends practicality with a well-appointed interior and a comprehensive suite of features.

title: I drove the fastest NEW electric cars until they DIED!
description:
Save up to £3750 on an EV with the Electric Car Grant: https://bit.ly/Electric-Car-Grant-2025

It’s time for another Carwow EV range test!

We’ve got our hands on three of the most powerful EVs that were available at the time of filming - the Tesla Model S Plaid, Lotus Emeya R, and the Audi RS e-tron GT Performance! The question is, which will go further? 

Well, we’re going to put the three to the test as we travel up the M1 motorway. Given these are the performance versions of each car, we’ll be driving them in sports mode, although we may switch to range/eco modes once each car is almost depleted of battery life. 

So, how do they compare? Starting with the Tesla, it’s powered by a 97 kWh battery and three electric motors. These create 1,020 hp, and the car has a claimed range of 364 miles. As new, it costs around £110,000. 

The Lotus Emeya R has dual motors, and these can produce 918hp. It has a 99kWh battery which is good for a claimed range of 301 miles, and as new it cost £131,000. 

Finally we have the Audi. It also has two motors, as well as a 97kWh battery pack. The motors help it produce 925hp, and it has a claimed range of 373 miles. It’s the most expensive car in this line-up, starting at £142,000. 

So which will travel the further - the Audi has the largest claimed range, but could it be beat by the Tesla or Lotus? You’ll have to stick with Mat and the team to see for yourself!

title: I Ranked Electric Cars by $/KM… The Winner Makes NO Sense!
description:
Which is the best BANG FOR BUCK EV in Australia? I've ranked them! Subscribe here: www.youtube.com/@therightcar?sub_confirmation=1

This video shows you the best electric cars when it comes to the least money spent to get the most EV range you can.

The idea is simple. Divide the cost of the car by the WLTP rated EV driving range -- that gives you a dollars per kilometre of range calculation.

All 15 cars on this list have a $/km cost of less than $100 -- and the winner has an ASTOUNDINGLY LOW cost for the amount of EV RANGE it offers!!

The pricing is based on 2000 postcode, and includes vehicles with current promotional drive-away pricing deals and introductory pricing on some models in this list.

The range figure is calculated wholly on the WLTP numbers that the manufacturers state.

title: TVS iQube ST 5.1 kWh Electric Scooter Review | 150 km Range & Advanced Features
description:
Discover the future of urban commuting with the TVS iQube ST 5.1 kWh electric scooter. Equipped with a robust 5.1 kWh lithium-ion battery, this scooter offers an impressive real-world range of 150 km per charge, ensuring longer rides without frequent charging.

Powered by a 3 kW BLDC hub-mounted motor, the iQube ST delivers a peak power of 4.4 kW and accelerates from 0 to 40 km/h in just 4.5 seconds, reaching a top speed of 82 km/h. 

The scooter features a 7-inch touchscreen TFT display, providing intuitive access to over 118 connected features, including turn-by-turn navigation, remote charge status, and voice assist. With 32 liters of under-seat storage, it comfortably accommodates two helmets, adding to its practicality.

Charging the iQube ST is convenient, achieving 0-80% charge in approximately 4 hours and 18 minutes. Safety features include geo-fencing, anti-theft alerts, and regenerative braking. The scooter is available in stylish colors like Copper Bronze Matte, Titanium Grey Matte, and Starlight Blue Glossy, allowing riders to choose a look that suits their personality. 

Experience the blend of performance, technology, and style with the TVS iQube ST 5.1 kWh electric scooter. Whether you're navigating city streets or embarking on longer journeys, the iQube ST is designed to make your ride efficient and enjoyable.

Chery has unveiled its next-generation Rhino solid-state battery that could deliver more than 1,500 km of driving range on a single charge. The company says the battery is targeting an energy density of up to 600 Wh/kg, which is far higher than current lithium-ion batteries used in most EVs today.

One of the biggest highlights is ultra-fast charging. Chery claims the Rhino battery can add up to 500 km of range in just 8 minutes. The battery is also designed for around 5,000 charging cycles, which could make it last much longer than current EV batteries.

Chery plans to begin testing the all-solid-state battery in the Exeed ES8 from 2027. Before that, semi-solid-state versions are expected to debut in other Chery and Exeed models later in 2026.

The Rhino battery family includes hybrid, fully electric, and future solid-state versions. Chery is investing heavily in this technology with more than 1,200 battery researchers and over RMB 10 billion in funding.

title: Summer mein EV Range kyun gir rahi hai?  #evkireport #evindia #electriccar #ytshorts
description:
Summer mein EV Range kyun gir rahi hai? ☀️📉
Agar aapki EV ki range 15-20% kam ho gayi hai, toh tension mat lo
aapki gaadi kharab nahi hai, ye sirf Physics hai! 🧠

Summer mein range girne ke 3 bade reasons aur unke "Real Life" fixes:

1️⃣ The AC Load: AC seedha battery se power leta hai. 
HACK: Gaadi plugged-in ho tabhi App se cabin thanda kar lo (Pre-cooling). 

2️⃣ Battery Stress: Garmi mein cooling system ko extra kaam karna padta hai. 
HACK: Dopahar 12-3 baje fast charging avoid karo aur hamesha shade mein park karo. 🌳

3️⃣ Tyre Friction: Naram tyres road pe zyaada friction banate hain. 
HACK: Recommended PSI maintain rakho (Kam mat karo!). 🛞

In fixes se aap range drop ko 8-10% tak bacha sakte ho. ✅
Aapki EV abhi kitni range de rahi hai? 👇 Niche comment mein batao!

No Hype. Only Real Life Auto Gyaan. 🧠

title: Why is everyone buying this car?
description:
Save up to £3750 on an EV with the Electric Car Grant: https://bit.ly/Electric-Car-Grant-2025

This is the all-new Renault 5! 

The original Renault 5 was released in the 1970s, but it went out of production way back in 1996. However, it’s now been resurrected in the form of an EV! The question, does the new Renault 5 do justice to the legacy of its predecessors? Let’s take a closer look…

Starting with the exterior design, there’s no denying that Renault has got it bang on! It’s modern yet retro, with several design cues taken from the design of the original Renault 5. Along the side, you get contrasting side mirrors and skirts, and there are different colour roofs available as well! At the back, there are contoured tail lights and fake vents that harp back to the Turbo 2. 

The retro-modern style continues through to the interior. There’s a dual digital dial-infotainment screen on the dash that looks smart and integrated, while there’s a striped design across the rest of the dash to help give it some definition. There may be some hard, scratchy plastics within the cabin, but you have to remember this is still an entry-level car. Thankfully, there’s also a selection of physical buttons on the centre console! The seats not only look retro and cool, but they’re comfortable as well! 

There are two power output choices for the Renault 5. The entry-level is the Urban, which delivers 120hp, 225Nm, and has a claimed 0-60 time of 9 seconds. It also has a claimed range of up to 190 miles. Then we have the Comfort, which delivers 150hp, 245Nm, and a 0-60 time of 7.9 seconds. The claimed range of this edition is 248 miles.

The entry-level Renault 5 starts at £23,000, rising to £29,000 for the range-topping edition. So is it the best small electric car you can buy for under £30,000? You’ll need to stick with Mat and see for yourself!

title: Jaecoo 7 review: The Chinese Range Rover
description:
Save up to £3750 on an EV with the Electric Car Grant: https://bit.ly/Electric-Car-Grant-2025

Introducing the Jaecoo 7!

It’s a mid-size SUV that originated from China, and at the time of writing it is the most configured car on Carwow! With that in mind, Mat’s got his hands on one to see what all the fuss is about. 

The key thing to mention is the price. Whereas competitor cars have seen their prices skyrocket in recent years, the Jaecoo comes in at a respective £30,000, rising to £35,000 for the plug-in hybrid. So when you couple that with a Carwow saving of around £2,500, the Jaecoo easily undercuts its key competitors! 

In terms of the design, it certainly holds a bold presence on the road, and if anything, is reminding us of a Range Rover! Up front, there’s a large, imposing grille which is flanked by headlights that come with graphics built in! Along the side, you’ll find pop-out door handles, and at the back, the car looks smart, with the windscreen wiper hidden at the top of the rear window - just like in a Range Rover! 

There’s a good amount of tech on the inside. All functions are handled through the large infotainment screen, but the system is pretty responsive and intuitive to use. There’s a digital dial screen, a heads up display as standard, and a good amount of storage throughout the cabin. The seats aren’t the most comfortable, but all-in-all it’s a nice place to be. 

There are two engines available: a 1.6-litre 4-cylinder turbo petrol that delivers 147hp and a 1.5-litre 4-cylinder plug-in hybrid that puts down 204hp. The former comes with AWD or FWD, while the PHEV is front-wheel drive only. 

So does the Jaecoo 7 offer enough to sway you away from established competitor brands? Or does Mat think you should stick with what you know? You’ll need to keep watching to see for yourself!

title: New Skoda Elroq Review
description:
Save up to £3750 on an EV with the Electric Car Grant: https://bit.ly/Electric-Car-Grant-2025

Meet the Skoda Elroq! 

It’s an all-new mid-sized electric SUV, and it’s meant to compete with class leaders such as the Kia EV3 and Hyundai Kona Electric. The question is, should the Elroq be at the top of your shopping list if you’re in the market for a car such as this? Let’s take a closer look. 

Regarding the design, the Elroq looks pretty stylish on the outside. Given it’s electric there’s no grille at the front but the split DRLs and headlights help give it presence, as do the grooves on the bonnet. Along the side the satin roof bars stand out to help give it some definition, while around the back it’s all pretty standard, but then again that’s no bad thing! 

Step inside and you might be surprised by how much room there is, especially for passengers in the back! Up front, the majority of the cabin is covered in soft-touch materials, and there’s a large infotainment screen on the dash. Unfortunately, the climate controls are managed through the screen, but at least the system is pretty responsive. There’s loads of storage to be found, and there’s also a digital driver display, although it’s relatively small. 

There are a couple of models available in the Elroq range. It kicks off with the Elroq 50, which is equipped with a single motor for 170hp, a 52kWh battery, and a claimed range of 232 miles. The model we’re reviewing here is the 85, which has a 77kWh battery, a claimed range of 355 miles, and a single motor.

The entry-level Elroq is priced from £31,510, rising up to around £40,000 for the top-of-the-range edition. So is it worth it? You’ll need to stick with Mat to see for yourself!

title: No Charging Needed in this Electric SUV ⚡️ Series Hybrid Car #hybrid #carguide #suv
description:
Range Extender Hybrid | Nissan Kicks e Power 2026 | Creta Hybrid & Duster Hybrid Rival ?

#NissanKicks #Hybrid #SeriesHybrid #ePower #Nissan #BIMS #CarGuide

Bangkok International Motor Show 2026 | New Nissan Kicks e Power 2026 is the perfect hybrid you need with a 1.2 Litre Petrol Engine acting as a generator to charge the 2 kWh battery to run the wheels. The engine does not power the wheels at all directly. No need to charge the battery of this vehicle but battery regen and battery charging via the generator helps the Nissan Kicks to achieve 25kmpl city mileage and around 18 to 20kmpl on highways.

Nissan Kicks e Power Hybrid rivals the upcoming Hyundai Creta 2027, Renault Duster Hybrid, Nissan Tekton Hybrid, Kia Seltos Hybrid 2027 and many more strong Hybrid cars like Maruti Suzuki Victoris, Maruti Suzuki Grand Vitara and Toyota Hyryder as well.

Este es uno de los anuncios más transformadores en la historia reciente de la movilidad eléctrica. CATL, el mayor fabricante de baterías del mundo, ha presentado su segunda generación de baterías de sodio: la Naxtra II.

¿Por qué esto es una "absoluta locura"? Porque estamos ante la tecnología que finalmente podría hacer que el coche eléctrico sea, de verdad, para todo el mundo.

¿Qué es exactamente una batería de sodio? 🔋

Para entender la importancia de la Naxtra II, primero debemos entender la diferencia fundamental con el litio. Imagina la batería como una autopista de iones (partículas cargadas).

En las baterías actuales, esos iones son de litio.
En las nuevas baterías de CATL, esos iones son de sodio.
El sodio tiene una ventaja imbatible: es extremadamente abundante. Se puede extraer literalmente de la sal del mar, lo que hace que los costes de producción sean órdenes de magnitud menores que los del litio, el cual es más escaso y complejo de minar.

Naxtra II: Rompiendo las barreras de la autonomía 🏁

Hasta ahora, el gran problema del sodio era su baja densidad energética. Sin embargo, con la Naxtra II, CATL ha dado un salto gigante:

Autonomías de entre 400 y 600 km: Cifras que ya igualan o superan a muchas baterías LFP (litio-ferrofosfato) actuales.
Eficiencia según el vehículo: Dependiendo de si se monta en un SUV o en un sedán aerodinámico, estas baterías permitirán viajes largos sin las limitaciones de las primeras generaciones de sodio.
Adiós a los problemas con el frío ❄️🔥

Uno de los puntos más flojos de los coches eléctricos actuales es el rendimiento en climas extremos. Las baterías de litio sufren mucho con las bajas temperaturas, perdiendo velocidad de carga y autonomía. La Naxtra II opera exactamente igual a -50ºC que a 30ºC. Esto elimina la necesidad de complejos sistemas de gestión térmica (bombas de calor, precalentamiento), lo que simplifica el diseño del coche y reduce aún más su precio.

¿Cuándo veremos estos coches en la calle? 🗓️

La revolución no es inmediata, pero ya está en marcha. CATL calcula que la producción a gran escala comenzará en el cuarto trimestre de 2026. Estamos ante la famosa "Curva S" de la producción masiva. Aunque ahora nos parezca lejano, en 24 a 36 meses el mercado de coches de gama media y baja se transformará radicalmente cuando los fabricantes sustituyan las costosas baterías LFP por estas de sodio.

Puntos clave del vídeo:

00:00 - Introducción: El anuncio que lo cambia todo.
01:14 - ¿Qué es una batería de sodio y en qué se diferencia del litio?
02:35 - La importancia del sodio para el almacenamiento estacionario y renovables.
03:30 - De la Naxtra 1 a la Naxtra 2: Evolución y densidad energética.
07:25 - Autonomías reales: ¿Podemos esperar 600 km con sodio?
10:30 - El fin de la ansiedad por la autonomía en invierno: Rendimiento a -50ºC.
12:15 - Seguridad extrema: Perforación sin incendios.
13:00 - El reto de la fabricación a gran escala (La Curva S).
15:45 - La inversión millonaria de CATL en I+D.

title: Renault C15 : Le moteur essence d'appoint pour voitures électriques
description:
L'industrie automobile est-elle tombée dans son propre piège ? Alors que les ventes de voitures 100 % électriques n'atteignent pas les niveaux espérés, Renault dégaine une arme secrète inattendue : le C15.
Développé par Horse, la filiale née du partenariat entre Renault, Geely et le géant pétrolier Aramco, ce nouveau moteur C15 X Range Direct Drive est une véritable révolution. Il a été pensé pour être installable sur n'importe quelle voiture électrique afin de la transformer en hybride ! Attention, il ne s'agit pas d'un simple générateur pour recharger la batterie : ce moteur thermique 4 cylindres de 1,5 L (disponible en 95 ch et en version turbo de 163 ch) est capable d'entraîner directement les roues.
Conçu pour être totalement "plug and play" et conseillé pour une installation à l'arrière des véhicules en raison des contraintes d'échappement, il offre une solution clé en main aux constructeurs. Plus besoin de dépenser des milliards ou d'attendre des années pour modifier les plateformes exclusivement électriques existantes.

title: Leapmotor C10 hybrid 2025 review: New range-extender electric car targets BYD Sealion 6 PHEV
description:
The 2025 Leapmotor C10 is already doubling down on its attack on Australia's mid-size SUV segment with a new powertrain option, dubbed REEV. It stands for Range Extending Electric Vehicle, meaning it introduces an internal-combustion engine to extend the driving range of the otherwise fully electric car.

Confused? Don't be. The new C10 REEV combines a 1.5-litre petrol four-cylinder unit with a 158kW electric motor. The former acts as a power generator for the 28.4kWh battery pack that can also be charged via a plug, while the latter exclusively drives the wheels. The end result is a NEDC-rated driving range of 1150km and an average fuel consumption of just 0.9L/100km.

In this expert review, CarsGuide Contributing Journalist Byron Mathioudakis travels to Spain to get an early taste of the C10 REEV before it arrives Down Under. Point being, is it worth waiting for over a BYD Sealion 6 PHEV? Watch on to find out.

title: New Omoda 9 review: Cancel your Audi Q5!
description:
Save up to £3750 on an EV with the Electric Car Grant: https://bit.ly/Electric-Car-Grant-2025

If you haven’t heard of Omoda before, you’re likely to hear much more of them in the future! Omoda is a division of the Chinese brand Chery, which is China’s fourth biggest car maker. They sold an incredible 2,600,000 cars in 2024 - more than Seat, Peugeot and Skoda combined! 

Now Chery is looking to expand into Europe, which has led us to get our hands on the all-new Omoda 9. It’s a roomy SUV packed full of tech, but does it offer enough to compete against established brands like Audi, BMW & Kia? Let’s take a closer look. 

In terms of the exterior design, it looks pretty smart! Up front, there’s a full-length light bar, vertical DRLs, and a large checkered grille effect on the front. Along the side, there are pop-out door handles, and if anything, it looks fairly similar to a Lexus RX - and that’s no bad thing! Around the back, it’s giving Nissan Qashqai vibes, along with a full-length light bar, and annoyingly fake exhaust pipes! But all in, this is a good-looking car. 

The good design features continue inside as well! There are soft-touch materials throughout the cabin, along with a large infotainment and digital dial screen. The infotainment system is packed with technology and features, and it’s quite responsive and easy to use. There are physical dials for the climate control, and there’s even a 14-speaker sound system, including speakers in the headrest! 

There’s only one engine available for the Omoda 9 - a 1.5-litre turbo petrol. However, it’s a plug in hybrid, and it comes with two electric motors and a 34kWh battery! As a result, it has four wheel drive, can produce a whopping 449hp and 700Nm, and it can drive for up to 90 miles on electric power alone! In fact, Omoda claims that due to the large fuel tank and battery, the Omodo 9 should be able to travel for up to 700 miles before you need to stop! 

The Omoda 9 costs from £44,990. At this price point, are you willing to consider it over more established European brands? Stick with Mat to see if you should!

title: 2025 Kia Carens Clavis EV 7-Seater Electric MPV - 490 Km Range, New Features, Interiors, Price | KIA
description:
The Kia Carens Clavis EV is Kia India's first locally manufactured electric vehicle ( Made in India ), designed as a mass-market 7-seater MPV. It aims to offer spaciousness, versatility, and electric mobility for families. Bookings officially opened on July 22, 2025.

Here's a detailed description:

Futuristic Design and Exterior:

EV-specific enhancements: While retaining the overall silhouette of the ICE Carens, the Clavis EV features a subtly redesigned front bumper with active aero flaps for improved efficiency, and the charging port is repositioned to the front.

Lighting: It sports new "ice-cube-style" LED fog lamps, a sleek full-width LED light bar across the front, and "Star Map LED Connected DRLs" that also serve as turn signals. The rear also gets revised LED taillamps with a connecting light bar.


Wheels: Aero-optimized 17-inch alloy wheels contribute to efficiency and a distinct EV look.

Frunk: A useful 25-litre front trunk (frunk) provides additional storage.

Interior and Features:

Spacious Cabin: Designed as a 7-seater, the Carens Clavis EV emphasizes comfort and practicality for families.

Dual-Tone Theme: The cabin generally features a modern black and beige dual-tone theme, with higher variants offering a Triton Navy and Beige leatherette upholstery.

Dual Panoramic Displays: A highlight is the 67.62 cm (26.62") Dual Panoramic Display Panel, integrating a 12.25-inch touchscreen for infotainment and a 12.25-inch digital driver's display.

Advanced Controls: It includes a touch panel for infotainment and AC controls with a "swap switch" for easy toggling, paddle shifters for controlling regenerative braking and i-Pedal mode, and a floating center console with storage and cupholders.

Comfort & Convenience:

One-touch electric tumble for second-row seats (for easy third-row access).

Roof-flushed AC vents for the second and third rows with four fan speeds.

64-color ambient lighting and footwell lamps (in higher variants).

Panoramic sunroof (in higher variants).

Ventilated front seats and a 4-way powered driver's seat (in higher variants).

Wireless charging for both rows.

Built-in cabin air purifier with AQI display.

Rear sunshades.

8-speaker Bose sound system (in higher variants).

Vehicle-to-Load (V2L) technology for powering external devices.

Wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto.

Performance and Range:

Powertrain Options: The Carens Clavis EV is offered with two battery pack options:

42 kWh unit: Offers a claimed ARAI range of 404 km.

51.4 kWh unit: Offers a claimed ARAI range of 490 km.

Motor: Powered by a front-axle mounted electric motor producing 255 Nm of torque. Power output varies by battery pack: 133 bhp (42 kWh) and 169 bhp (51.4 kWh).

Acceleration: Capable of 0-100 kmph in approximately 8.4 seconds (for the larger battery).

Charging: Supports 100 kW DC fast charging, allowing a 10% to 80% charge in approximately 39 minutes. It also supports 11 kW AC charging (10-100% in ~4 hours).


Regenerative Braking: Features a four-stage regenerative braking system controlled by paddle shifters, including an i-Pedal one-pedal driving mode.

Safety:

Standard Safety: Comes standard with 6 airbags, ESC, ABS, Hill-Start Assist Control (HAC), Tyre Pressure Monitoring System (TPMS), and all-wheel disc brakes.

Advanced Safety (Higher Variants):

Level 2 ADAS with 20 autonomous features, including Forward Collision Warning, Lane Keeping Assist, Smart Cruise Control with Stop and Go, Blind Spot Collision Avoidance Assist, and Rear Cross-Traffic Collision Avoidance Assist.

360-degree camera.

Blind View Monitor in the instrument cluster.

Virtual engine sound system for pedestrian safety.

Variants and Pricing (Ex-showroom, India):

HTK+ (42 kWh): Rs. 17.99 lakh

HTX (42 kWh): Rs. 20.49 lakh

HTX ER (51.4 kWh): Rs. 22.49 lakh

HTX+ ER (51.4 kWh): Rs. 24.49 lakh

Introducing the Carens Clavis EV – It’s an E.We.

The Kia Carens Clavis EV positions itself as a practical and feature-rich electric MPV, catering to the growing demand for electric family vehicles in India.

title: This Changes Everything! | Up to 450 km of Range | 2026 Volkswagen ID.Polo | AutoNext
description:
Meet the new Volkswagen ID.Polo.

Volkswagen is bringing one of its most familiar names into the electric era, and it could become a very important model for the brand. Compact dimensions, everyday practicality and an all electric platform designed for the mass market. This is exactly the kind of EV many buyers have been waiting for.

The challenge is clear. Keep the simplicity and accessibility that made the Polo so successful, while making the switch to electric feel like a genuine upgrade rather than a compromise.

If Volkswagen gets this right, the ID.Polo could become one of the most relevant small EVs in Europe.

title: Wuling Cloud EV Claim 460km, Segini Real Range Jakarta-Bandung
description:
Wuling Cloud EV claim range 460 km. Tapi gimana 
real range-nya setelah dipake Jakarta-Bandung 
plus muter dalam kota?

Di video ini gue catet semua data baterai dari 
Jakpus → Bandung → SPKLU TSM. Total tempuh 170 km, 
3-4 penumpang, AC nyala normal — apa adanya, 
no editing data.

📊 DATA REAL TRIP:
🔋 Start: Baterai 99% | Range 455 km
🛣️ Exit KCIC Halim: 96% | 440 km
🛣️ KM 72 Tol Cipali: 78% | 356 km
🛣️ Exit KCIC Padalarang: 59% | 270 km
📍 SPKLU TSM Bandung: 51% | 230 km

📈 KESIMPULAN:
• 170 km tempuh = 49% baterai habis
• Real konsumsi: ~3.5 km / 1% baterai
• Proyeksi real range full charge: ±350 km
• Gap claim vs real: ±110 km

🚗 KONTEKS PERJALANAN:
• 3 penumpang (Jakpus-Padalarang)
• 4 penumpang (Bandung in-city)
• AC fan 1, temp 2 (normal cool)
• Mix: tol Cipali, MBZ bumpy, dalam kota Bandung

🔌 Charging di SPKLU TSM sambil meeting — 
sampe sore dapet 84%.

📌 Video terkait:
👉 Review jujur kursi belakang Wuling Cloud EV 
   buat penumpang tinggi 180cm — udah ada di channel

Konten review jujur — bukan promosi, bukan 
hate. Just real data buat lo yang lagi 
pertimbangin Cloud EV sebagai daily atau 
long trip vehicle.

title: 3 Hours in Tata Punch EV with AC ON! Battery Drain Test in Real Life 😃🚙✅ #evcar #tatapunchev #viral
description:
